L1.area: Connection data reading and frame processing

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the WebSocket frame length parsing. When processing incoming frames, the payload length value read from the wire is converted from an unsigned to a signed integer without checking for overflow. An attacker can send crafted WebSocket frames with large payload length values that wrap to negative when converted, bypassing the connection's read limit and causing denial of service.

L3.area: WebSocket frame advancement and message reading within the connection handler
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the WebSocket frame length parsing. When a frame's payload length indicator signals a 64-bit length, the unsigned 64-bit value read from the wire is cast directly to a signed 64-bit integer without overflow validation. A malicious peer can send frames with payload lengths that overflow the signed integer, causing length tracking variables to wrap negative, bypassing the read limit check and enabling denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: An integer overflow in WebSocket frame length parsing allows a malicious peer to send frames with payload lengths that overflow a signed 64-bit integer, bypassing the read limit and causing denial of service.
